somtimes there is error to captcha

content.text:
A few things to try for captcha errors:

**1. Use Android/iOS client (most effective)**
```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies cookies.txt \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=android" \
    --sleep-requests 2 \
    --sleep-interval 5 \
    --max-sleep-interval 15 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```

The Android/iOS client typically bypasses captcha challenges entirely.

**2. If that still fails, also add:**
```fish
--extractor-args "youtube:player_client=ios,android"
```

**3. Make sure cookies are fresh** — stale cookies are the #1 cause. Export them again from a browser where you're actively logged into YouTube/YouTube Music. Use the "Get cookies.txt LOCALLY" extension (Chrome/Firefox), not an online tool.

**4. Increase delays further:**
```fish
--sleep-requests 3 \
--sleep-interval 10 \
--max-sleep-interval 30 \
```

**5. Keep yt-dlp updated:**
```fish
yt-dlp -U
```

The `--extractor-args "youtube:player_client=android"` fix is usually sufficient on its own since YouTube's bot detection is much lighter on the mobile API endpoints.

content.text:
Your `cookies.txt` is likely stale or invalid. The easiest fix is to let yt-dlp read directly from your browser instead:

```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies-from-browser firefox \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=android,ios" \
    --sleep-requests 3 \
    --sleep-interval 10 \
    --max-sleep-interval 30 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```

Replace `firefox` with `chrome`, `chromium`, or `brave` depending on what you use — and make sure you're logged into YouTube in that browser.

If you prefer to keep using `cookies.txt`, export it fresh using the browser extension **"Get cookies.txt LOCALLY"** while logged into YouTube, then use `--cookies cookies.txt` again.

content.text:
The issue is yt-dlp needs Node.js to solve YouTube's JS challenges (deno is failing). Install it:

```fish
sudo pacman -S nodejs
```

Then run the same command again. yt-dlp will automatically use Node.js instead of deno.

If you already have Node.js installed, update yt-dlp first:

```fish
yt-dlp -U
```

Then retry the download command.

content.text:
The solver script is outdated (0.7.0 vs required 0.8.0). Add `--remote-components ejs:github` to fetch the latest one:

```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies-from-browser brave \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=android,ios" \
    --remote-components ejs:github \
    --sleep-requests 3 \
    --sleep-interval 10 \
    --max-sleep-interval 30 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```

content.text:
Two problems:
1. `android`/`ios` clients don't work with cookies — they get skipped
2. `web_music` needs a PO Token

Switch to the `web` client which supports cookies and doesn't need a PO Token:

```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies-from-browser brave \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=web" \
    --remote-components ejs:github \
    --sleep-requests 3 \
    --sleep-interval 10 \
    --max-sleep-interval 30 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```

content.text:
The bundled EJS scripts in your yt-dlp installation are outdated (0.7.0 vs required 0.8.0). Your yt-dlp itself needs updating. Since you're on Arch, reinstall via the official binary which has the latest scripts:

```fish
sudo curl -L https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/latest/download/yt-dlp -o /usr/local/bin/yt-dlp
sudo chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/yt-dlp
```

Then verify the version:
```fish
yt-dlp --version
```

And retry the download. The new binary bundles EJS 0.8.0 which should fix the JS challenge errors.

message.content: WARNING: [youtube] j34caPfxZt8: web_music client https formats require a GVS PO Token which was not provided. They will be skipped as they may yield HTTP Error 403. You can manually pass a GVS PO Token for this client with --extractor-args "youtube:po_token=web_music.gvs+XXX". For more information, refer to  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/wiki/PO-Token-Guide

content.text:
Is the download actually proceeding despite the warning, or is it still erroring? The warning just means web_music formats are skipped — if the `web` client has audio formats available, it should still download fine.

If it's still failing, you can get a PO Token using the `yt-dlp-get-pot` plugin:

```fish
pip install yt-dlp-get-pot --break-system-packages
```

Then run with:

```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies-from-browser brave \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=web" \
    --remote-components ejs:github \
    --sleep-requests 3 \
    --sleep-interval 10 \
    --max-sleep-interval 30 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```

The plugin auto-generates the PO Token from your browser session when `--cookies-from-browser` is set.

content.text:
Install pipx first, then use it:

```fish
sudo pacman -S python-pipx
pipx install yt-dlp-get-pot
```

content.text:
`yt-dlp-get-pot` is just a framework — it needs a provider plugin. That's complex to set up. Let's skip it and try the `tv` client instead, which doesn't need a PO Token:

```fish
yt-dlp \
    -f bestaudio \
    --extract-audio \
    --audio-format mp3 \
    --embed-thumbnail \
    --cookies-from-browser brave \
    --extractor-args "youtube:player_client=tv" \
    --sleep-requests 3 \
    --sleep-interval 10 \
    --max-sleep-interval 30 \
    -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" \
    "https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLIYzRoHjcOlN1qd4aeuC7kXU-U_ZVGcwk"
```
